items.title: NT Labor MLA pledges change after another speeding fine revelation

children.content: An NT Labor politician has conceded he has an issue with speeding and will take driver refresher training, as a further infringement comes to light.

gallery.altText: An Aboriginal man, with black hair, sitting in a office chair in parliament, with a navy blue suit on, sullen expression.

thumbnail.altText: An Aboriginal man, with black hair, sitting in a office chair in parliament, with a navy blue suit on, sullen expression.

items.title: Millions spent on hardline school policies, but NT students not turning up

children.content: Just over half of all Aboriginal students in the NT are showing up for school, despite millions being spent on truancy policies.

image.altText: A group of primary aged kids in school uniforms walking on a red dirt road.

items.title: Qantas axes Alice Springs-Melbourne route amid high cost, falling demand

children.content: Qantas has axed its direct Alice Springs-Melbourne route after "many decades", and will also restrict flights between Darwin and Singapore to the peak tourist season.

image.altText: A photo of a Qantas plane flying in the sky.

items.title: NT outlaw bikie gang president sentenced to 10 years in prison

children.content: Secretly taped phone calls have captured the former president of the Mongols bikie gang's Northern Territory chapter discussing interstate drug deals while helping supply methamphetamine worth almost $800,000 to customers across the Top End.

gallery.altText: A police officer holds up a black vest reading Mongols MC Australia.

thumbnail.altText: A police officer holds up a black vest reading Mongols MC Australia.

items.title: Senior firefighter claims boss labelled her 'an overly emotional woman'

children.content: A former senior Northern Territory firefighter has claimed the service's deputy chief labelled her "an overly emotional woman" in a complaint to the territory's Anti-Discrimination Commission.

image.altText: A man in a dark blue uniform smiles for the camera.

items.title: Mobile CT scanner visits region where 'at least one smoker in every house'

children.content: A road train fitted with a battery-powered CT scanner is travelling across the NT outback to screen remote community residents for lung cancer, in the jurisdiction with Australia's highest diagnosis and mortality rates for the disease.

image.altText: A white road train parked in a parking lot with starts coming out of its side, and shelter. Sign reads 'Heart of Australia'

items.title: NT Labor MLA caught speeding multiple times, years after fatal crash conviction

children.content: Manuel Brown had his licence suspended for three months last October, which was not disclosed publicly at the time. The Arafura MLA also failed to disclose a conviction over a fatal crash when he was initially preselected for Labor in 2023.

image.altText: Man in suit sits on chair and puts pen in mouth

items.title: Matt Wright sued for negligence after airboat allegedly crashed into tree

children.content: A man who says he suffered a "psychiatric injury" after witnessing injuries sustained by his wife in a 2023 airboat crash is suing tour operator and Netflix star Matt Wright for negligence.

image.altText: A man in a button-up shirt and jeans, walking towards the courthouse while holding his wife's hand.

items.title: One CDU employee has been back paid $242k amid $4m stolen wages bill

children.content: Charles Darwin University (CDU) has been directed to back pay more than $4 million in wages after it was found to have underpaid more than 800 professional and academic staff members between 2016 and 2022.

image.altText: CDU sign

items.title: Second Mongols bikie to escape custody in the NT caught in eight minutes

children.content: A "known associate" of the Mongols outlaw bikie gang has been arrested after fleeing custody at Royal Darwin Hospital — the second time a Mongols member has fled custody in the Northern Territory this month.

image.altText: The side door of a NT Police car.

items.title: Ex-CEO vindicated in $2.3 million court ruling against space firm

children.content: Once a promising space start-up chief executive, Carley Scott was terminated from her job and left millions out of pocket. Now she's finally found vindication, with a court ruling in her favour.

gallery.altText: A woman with a brown ponytail wearing hi-vis smiles at a worksite
A rocket launching into the air, at night.
A woman in an orange top speaks into a microphone at a press conference.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with a brown ponytail wearing hi-vis smiles at a worksite
A rocket launching into the air, at night.
A woman in an orange top speaks into a microphone at a press conference.

items.title: UN pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation after ship reports attack

children.content: The UN pauses its operation to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz after a vessel reports an attack.

image.altText: A ship in the forground and another moving a long the horizon the background.

items.title: Another suspected H5 bird flu case detected in WA

children.content: Another suspected positive case of the H5 bird flu has been detected in Western Australia, marking the fifth confirmed or suspected case in Australia.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder-length hair is speaking to media.

items.title: How China's new 'ethnic unity' law could target people in Australia

children.content: The Australian government raises concerns directly with China over the country's new ethnic unity law, which allows Beijing to target people overseas.

gallery.altText: Three women in ethnic minority outfits smile outside the Great Hall of the People.
10 people stand side by side wearing different ethnic minority outfits.

thumbnail.altText: Three women in ethnic minority outfits smile outside the Great Hall of the People.
10 people stand side by side wearing different ethnic minority outfits.

items.title: 'ISIS bride' charged with slavery offences is granted bail

children.content: A court grants bail for Melbourne grandmother Kawsar Ahmad, who is alleged to have owned a slave while in Syria under Islamic State rule.

image.altText: A woman wearing a brown hijab sits in court.

items.title: Battle lost for Zempilas and WA Liberals in hope of winning war ahead

children.content: Basil Zempilas has had his first brush with the political truism "disunity is death" after MP Jonathan Huston quit the WA Liberal Party, but he's unlikely — at this stage at least — to be the one left in a political grave.

image.altText: Basil Zempilas is speaking to media with Jonathan Huston in the background.

items.title: 'An outright lie': Minister challenges Butler on Thriving Kids rollout

children.content: A member of the Thriving Kids advisory panel has voiced concern about how the support program for children with autism and developmental delay is being rolled out.

image.altText: A close up of a kindergartener's hands on a table

items.title: Man sentenced for 'execution' of police officer serving repossession notice

children.content: Tasmanian man Leigh Sushames shot dead Keith Smith last year when the police constable tried to serve him with a home repossession notice.

image.altText: Leigh Sushames looks at the camera.

items.title: No-show cuttlefish result in million-dollar loss for regional SA town

children.content: Whyalla has been forced to wind back services and let staff let go as its tourism industry suffers million-dollar losses from cuttlefish not making their usual migration.

gallery.altText: Four Giant Australian cuttlefish are seen close up at a low angle under water. they are purple and grey in colour.

thumbnail.altText: Four Giant Australian cuttlefish are seen close up at a low angle under water. they are purple and grey in colour.

items.title: Daughter arrested after mother's body found at Sydney aged care home

children.content: Police question a 53-year-old woman in Sydney's west about an allegedly administered substance after her mother was found dead in an aged care home.

image.altText: Darrin wears a police hat and uniform and speaks into media microphones.

items.title: How I save money by storing and cooking things differently

children.content: I've developed simple storage and cooking systems that keep food visible and fresh while ensuring I use things up. Here's how I make cooking for one feel more doable, affordable and lower-impact.

image.altText: Koren stands, smiling, in front of her pantry with glass bottles behind her stacked up

avatar.alt: A woman sits in her front garden, which she replanted with native plants to attract local butterflies and insects.

items.title: Dwayne Johnson and Catherine Lagaʻaia hit the Moana blue carpet

children.content: Dwayne Johnson and Sydneysider Catherine Lagaʻaia have walked the blue carpet at a special event in Sydney. They told ABC News why now is the perfect time for a live action Moana.

gallery.altText: Catherine Lagaʻaia and Dwayne Johnson standing in front of a Moana sign with flowers and plants surrounding them

thumbnail.altText: Catherine Lagaʻaia and Dwayne Johnson standing in front of a Moana sign with flowers and plants surrounding them

items.title: Supergirl shoots for the moon, but runs out of fuel

children.content: Australian Milly Alcock shines as Supergirl in the hero's first solo outing since 1984. If only the rest of the movie sparkled around her.

image.altText: Supergirl flying

items.title: Comparing Trump's Iran negotiations to what Obama already did

children.content: The Trump administration has been negotiating directly with Iran this week in an effort to reach a long-term peace agreement, but it has several hurdles to clear before it can better the deal the United States already had.

image.altText: A composite image of the two leaders in suits

items.title: Friday news quiz: Neil the Seal, the teals party down and Trump drains his swamp

children.content: This week's news quiz features the usual assortment of political animals and regular animals, as well as other odd happenings from across the world.

gallery.altText: Two people attempt to force a seal off a road.

thumbnail.altText: Two people attempt to force a seal off a road.

items.title: ASX down as ACCC announces anti-price gouging law

children.content: The ASX has slipped into negative territory after lunch on Friday. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: An electronic billboard showing stock prices is seen in a window reflection.

items.title: Warning fire ants could kill thousands of pets a year

children.content: If Australia does not ramp up fire ant eradication efforts, the invasive species will cause billion-dollar economic impacts and dozens of deaths, a study finds.

image.altText: A fire ant nest

items.title: H5N1 emerged in Asia 30 years ago. Here's what happened next

children.content: Experts say Asia's decades-long experience fighting bird flu has shown there is no magic bullet and that fragmented responses to the disease will fail.

gallery.altText: A farmer in overalls feeds a flock of chickens in an enclosure.

thumbnail.altText: A farmer in overalls feeds a flock of chickens in an enclosure.

items.title: Queensland to introduce new drink and drug driving laws to fix 'loophole'

children.content: The Crisafulli government has announced a crackdown on dangerous driving while doubling down on a "zero-tolerance" approach to drug driving, including for medical cannabis users.

image.altText: Deb Frecklington on a police motorbike.

items.title: Labor frontbencher Grace Grace apologises after committee's contempt finding

children.content: The parliament's ethics committee made the decision after investigating "quarrelling" between Grace Grace and Deputy Premier, Jarrod Bleijie, during a sitting week last year.

image.altText: A woman in a red shirt with black jacket crossing her fingers at a podium.

items.title: 'Incredibly special moment': Australian underdog makes it to Wimbledon

children.content: Australia's Dane Sweeny makes it through to an overseas grand slam for the first time after a hard-fought victory in the Wimbledon qualifiers.

gallery.altText: A tennis player pumps his fist and screams in joy after winning a point.

thumbnail.altText: A tennis player pumps his fist and screams in joy after winning a point.

items.title: The Dolphins-Warriors game is the NRL's unlikeliest blockbuster

children.content: In a season bereft of quality, the Dolphins and the Warriors will meet in a marquee clash that doubles as the NRL's most unusual big game showdown.

image.altText: A rugby league player runs the ball during a match

items.title: Essendon announces selection panel to pick next AFL coach

children.content: Essendon has opted to go in-house with the panel that will decide whether James Hird returns as its AFL senior coach.

image.altText: James Hird, Director of Coaching at Port Melbourne speaks to players at the huddle

items.title: Man suing Spirit of Tasmania operator told he 'should have sat' on ship's toilet

children.content: A man who claims to have sustained injuries after falling over while using a toilet at sea says the ferry operator was negligent, the federal court has heard.

image.altText: A large white and yellow cruise ship sits in the waterfront of a city.

items.title: Matthew Guy says he's owed apology over Moira Deeming assault allegation

children.content: Victoria Police close an investigation into allegations state MP Matthew Guy assaulted fellow Liberal Moira Deeming at a community event, determining there was "there was no offence detected".

image.altText: A man in a suit stands looking sad.
